name: Circular por Neila y sus Lagunas — circular por el Parque natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila
region_intro: Road cycling around Salas De Los Infantes offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de la Demanda region of Burgos, Spain. The area features a mix of rebollo oak forests, dense pine forests, meadows, and river valleys, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Elevation changes are present, including mountain passes and routes near glacial lagoons, making it suitable for different levels of road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Salas De Los Infantes The most popular road cycling route is Circular por Neila y sus Lagunas — circular por el Parque natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila, a 61.5 miles (99.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 1 minute to complete. This difficult route leads through the Parque Natural de las Lagunas Glaciares de Neila. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de la Demanda Loop from Salas de los Infantes, a difficult 46.9 miles (75.4 km) path. This route offers varied terrain within the broader Sierra de la Demanda mountain range. Local road cyclists also love the Salas de los Infantes - Cañón del Río Lobos Loop, a 55.6 miles (89.5 km) trail leading through the scenic Cañón del Río Lobos, often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes. Road cycling around Salas De Los Infantes is defined by dense pine forests, rebollo oak forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for both moderate and challenging ability levels. The routes in Salas De Los Infantes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Salas De Los Infantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Salas de los Infantes?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes around Salas de los Infantes, offering diverse options for cyclists. These routes range in difficulty, with a good mix of moderate and challenging paths through the Sierra de la Demanda region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Salas de los Infantes?
A: The region offers a varied landscape for road cycling. You'll encounter rebollo oak forests, dense pine forests, open meadows, and picturesque river valleys. Expect elevation changes, including mountain passes like the Puerto del Manquillo, and routes that lead near glacial lagoons, providing a dynamic cycling experience.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Salas de los Infantes?
A: Yes, Salas de los Infantes is well-suited for challenging road cycling. Many routes feature significant climbs and elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056055738/circular-por-neila-y-sus-lagunas-circular-por-el-parque-natural-lagunas-glaciares-de-neila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular por Neila y sus Lagunas&lt;/a&gt; route is classified as difficult, covering nearly 100 km with over 1600 meters of ascent. The Puerto del Manquillo also presents an 11-kilometer climb with gradients up to 11%.

Q: Are there any easier or family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: While many road cycling routes in the immediate guide are classified as moderate to difficult, the region does offer more relaxed options. The 'Greenway of the Sierra de la Demanda' is a notable feature, transforming an old railway line into a varied bike tour with gentle slopes, making it suitable for a more relaxed pace or family outings. This route is not specifically listed in the road cycling guide, but it's a great alternative for those seeking less challenging terrain.

Q: What are some longer road cycling routes in the Salas de los Infantes region?
A: For longer rides, you have several excellent options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056055738/circular-por-neila-y-sus-lagunas-circular-por-el-parque-natural-lagunas-glaciares-de-neila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular por Neila y sus Lagunas&lt;/a&gt; route spans approximately 99 km. Another extensive option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Neila Lagoons – Direction Puerto El Collado loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 104 km long. These routes provide substantial distances for endurance cyclists.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Salas de los Infantes?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in the area are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056055738/circular-por-neila-y-sus-lagunas-circular-por-el-parque-natural-lagunas-glaciares-de-neila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular por Neila y sus Lagunas&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056654646/sierra-de-la-demanda-loop-from-salas-de-los-infantes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Demanda Loop from Salas de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056027287/salas-de-los-infantes-canon-del-rio-lobos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas de los Infantes - Cañón del Río Lobos Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I find along road cycling routes in the area?
A: The region offers several scenic viewpoints. You can enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Carazo Plateau&lt;/a&gt; or take in the unique landscape from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4997652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Manquillo&lt;/a&gt; mountain pass also provides stunning vistas as you climb through the pine forests.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to see while cycling near Salas de los Infantes?
A: Absolutely. The area is rich in history. You can cycle past the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt;, a famous filming location. The ruins of the Monasterio de San Pedro de Arlanza are also located along the Arlanza river, offering a glimpse into the region's monastic past. Salas de los Infantes itself hosts a Dinosaur Museum, showcasing local prehistoric finds.

Q: What natural attractions are near the road cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can explore routes that lead to significant natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056055738/circular-por-neila-y-sus-lagunas-circular-por-el-parque-natural-lagunas-glaciares-de-neila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular por Neila y sus Lagunas&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, takes you through the Parque Natural de las Lagunas Glaciares de Neila, known for its striking glacial lakes. The Cañón del Río Lobos also offers dramatic landscapes and is featured in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056027287/salas-de-los-infantes-canon-del-rio-lobos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas de los Infantes - Cañón del Río Lobos Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Salas de los Infantes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from dense pine forests to river valleys, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels. The quiet roads and immersive natural experience are frequently highlighted.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Salas de los Infantes?
A: The best time for road cycling in Salas de los Infantes is generally from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, making mountain passes more accessible and enjoyable. Summer offers warm conditions, ideal for exploring the higher elevations and enjoying the long daylight hours, though it's wise to start early to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer a good mix of scenery and challenge?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056654646/sierra-de-la-demanda-loop-from-salas-de-los-infantes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Demanda Loop from Salas de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for a balanced experience. This difficult 75.4 km route offers varied terrain within the broader Sierra de la Demanda mountain range, combining challenging sections with beautiful natural surroundings.
tips: Right when you pass by the town of Castrovido, there is an old stone bridge that was used in ancient times to cross the river that passes by the town itself.
Canales de la Sierra is a municipality and Spanish town belonging to the region of the 7 Villas. The town is located at the foot of the Sierra de la Demanda, which has large beech and pine forests.

Beautiful village of Burgos nestled in the hill of the source of the river Neila. From here you can see an impressive view of the surrounding valleys and infinite mountain ridges of the Sierra de la Demanda. It is an ideal place to make a stop to refuel after cycling up.

One of the most impressive places in the entire province of Burgos and where, in addition, the ascent is famous for the fact that the Vuelta Ciciclista a Burgos, its main stage, ends in this tough climb.
The starting point for the Lagunas Glaciares de Neila natural park. The economic activity of this town is logging (sawmills, furniture factories, carpentry workshops, extraction of pine resin, etc.).
